parsed_review.summary: CP00-695 is a descriptor-only RP23 External Integrations II domain-model/workflow-slice extension (40 units, RP23.P01.M02.S09-RP23.P01.M04.S06, risk B). Independent read-only inspection of registry.js, service.js, validators.js, index.js, test/model.test.js, the generated external-integrations-ii contract, the manifest, and the evidence files confirms the pack's claims. The implementation produces only Object.freeze'd descriptor objects with no runtime dispatch, no DB/object-storage access, no permission/audit evaluation, and no Hermes runtime receipts; FINANCE_INTEGRATIONS_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION flags are truthful (the only filesystem write is the dev-time contract projection artifact, outside product-state scope). Contract coverage and descriptor validation are clean (valid:true, errors:[]) for both CP00-694 (historical) and CP00-695 (latest); distributions match frozen requirements (impl 23 / ui 5 / fixture 2 / test 6 / hermes 2 / claude 2; M02 12 / M03 22 / M04 6). Six node:test cases exercise create/validate functions, distribution drift, authority boundaries, and handoff identity; command-evidence records the 23/23 pre-review validation matrix at exit 0 (including npm test, build, and git diff --check). Authority boundary is intact: claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, local validation does not claim enterprise trust, and Claude packets are read-only with hardened invalid-review blockers. Hermes patterns are descriptor/control-plane only (emits_runtime_receipt=false). The pack correctly stays production_ready=false in pending_review with no premature or invalid review receipt; readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON (1436-byte stdout, exit 0) — no auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed JSON, fenced/prose, or tool-call-shaped evidence. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; two P3 informational provenance notes, both dispositioned acceptable. Verdict: PASS — eligible for closeout adjudication. Final human/enterprise-trust approval remains outside this read-only receipt.

findings.title: CP00-695 plan binding resolves via manifest snapshot, not the live plan forward queue
findings.details: The live closeout-pack-plan.json packs[] forward queue begins at CP00-696 (line 401) while live_state.pack_id is CP00-695 (line 36), so model.test.js planPack() finds no CP00-695 entry in plan.packs and falls back to manifest.plan_binding_snapshot. I verified that snapshot is consistent with review-baseline.json source_plan_pack and registry FINANCE_INTEGRATIONS_CP695_REQUIREMENTS (40 units; impl 23/ui 5/fixture 2/test 6/hermes 2/claude 2; M02 12/M03 22/M04 6; range RP23.P01.M02.S09-RP23.P01.M04.S06). changed-file-scope.json appropriately lists the plan/queue files under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved.
findings.recommendation: No action required. The manifest plan_binding_snapshot is the authoritative frozen binding for a pack mid-closeout and the test fallback is intentional; provenance is sound.

findings.title: Sole filesystem write is the dev-time contract projection artifact, outside product no-write scope
findings.details: The validator writeFile()s contracts/external-integrations-ii-contract.json during npm run rp23:external-integrations-ii:validate. This is a deterministic control-plane/build artifact, not product runtime state: it does not create/update database rows, object storage, audit events, permission decisions, or Hermes runtime receipts. FINANCE_INTEGRATIONS_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (writes_product_state:false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, creates_database_rows:false, etc.) scopes to product state and remains truthful, consistent with the prior CP00-694 pattern.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Optionally document that no_write_attestation covers product state rather than dev-time generated artifacts to remove future ambiguity.
findings.post_review_disposition: Accepted; non-blocking and consistent with the imported deterministic control-plane review-packet/receipt pattern.
